definition: Hyperscaler OCF/capex ratio of 1.5-1.7x eliminates the debt-driven bust mechanism seen in prior tech cycles, converting potential crash risk to slow-grind duration risk over 3-7+ years. Historical analogues (Japan 1990s-2000s, energy majors 2010-2014) both produced slow grinds because no creditor-imposed stop-loss existed.
evidence: Japanese corporates 1990s-2000s: TOPIX underperformed MSCI World by ~60% over 13 years
Energy majors 2010-2014: P/E compressed from 14x to 8x over 2 years
No creditor-imposed stop-loss in either historical analogue
open_questions: Whether $40-60B/month international capital flows create a non-linearity absent in Japan/energy analogues
Whether US shareholder activism provides capex discipline mechanism altering the slow-grind pattern
N=2 for self-funded bust analogues is thin
